title: quercus boissieri subsp. tauricola
intro: Quercus boissieri subsp. tauricola, also known as the Dalmatian white oak, is a subspecies of evergreen tree native to the Balkan Peninsula. Its bark, leaves, and fruit have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, with medicinal properties attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Quercus boissieri subsp. tauricola include its potential to act as an antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial agent, which may help to protect against chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease, diabetes, and certain types of cancer. The plant is typically prepared for medicinal use in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas, capsules, or added to topical creams and ointments.
